title: Cyrtochilum stenochilum
taxonomy: Cyrtochilum stenochilum is a species of orchid in the genus Cyrtochilum. The genus Cyrtochilum belongs to the subtribe Cyrtochiliinae, which is a part of the tribe Maxillarieae. Cyrtochilum stenochilum is classified as a member of the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, one of which is the Epidendroideae subfamily. Cyrtochilum stenochilum's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Cyrtochilum stenochilum, also known as the "tongue orchid," is a species of orchid native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtochilum stenochilum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Cyrtochilum stenochilum is used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
